title: Stratospheric ozone concentration projections
subtitle: Stratospheric ozone concentrations with projections to 2100 based on chemistry-climate models. These are measured as global and regional averages relative to levels in 1960 (1960 = 0).
caption:
Stratospheric ozone concentration projections, 1960 to 2100

Stratospheric ozone concentrations with projections to 2100 based on chemistry-climate models. These are measured as global and regional averages relative to levels in 1960 (1960 = 0).

The line chart tracks stratospheric ozone concentrations from 1960 through 2100, presenting global and regional averages relative to 1960 baseline levels where 1960 equals zero. The vertical axis measures percentage deviation ranging from negative 140 to positive 40, while the horizontal axis spans the 140-year period. Three distinct trajectories appear: the Arctic region, the Global average, and the Antarctic region. The Arctic line begins near zero in 1960, rises slightly to approximately 5 by 1970, then declines to roughly negative 25 around 2000 before reversing direction and climbing steadily to reach approximately 40 by 2100, marking the highest endpoint among all three series. The Global average follows a similar but more moderate pattern, dipping to around negative 15 near 2000 and recovering to just above zero by the century's end. The Antarctic line displays the most extreme variation, plunging from zero in 1960 to approximately negative 120 around 2005, then ascending consistently to reach roughly negative 5 by 2100. All three lines show a common structural feature: decline through the late twentieth century followed by recovery in the twenty-first century. The Arctic region crosses above the Global average around 2025 and maintains the highest values thereafter. The figures represent the mean across a number of model runs; model projections have notable uncertainty around such average trends. The Antarctic trajectory exhibits the largest magnitude of change, with a range spanning approximately 115 percentage points from its lowest point to its 2100 endpoint.
